key_points: Dorm conditions historically poor; dirty bathrooms explicitly called out
Thieving roommates flagged in a viral honest campus tour video
Alumni note major upgrades since early 2000s, but baseline still rough
key_quotes: DIRTY BATHROOMS?? THIEVING ROOMMATES?
it was better than the Towers back in the early 90s. It was some nasty ass heffas in them dorms!!

key_points: Band culture and Greek life are the undisputed social heartbeat of campus
Stroll-offs, Battle of the Greeks, and homecoming are major recurring events
Social scene is vibrant but heavily tied to Greek orgs and band community

key_points: Almost zero student commentary on dining quality from either source
Campus tour notes a cafe in the library as an upgrade alumni appreciated
Dining workers rallied for unionization — suggests labor tension in food services

key_points: Counseling Center runs active programming including journaling groups and yoga
Self-Care Day events with free food signal institutional awareness of student stress
No student complaints or praise about counseling quality — usage unclear

Spring Refresher: Self-Care Day — Mental wellness tips, DIY self-care kits, journaling and vision boarding

Masters program in electronics engineering noted as unique in Virginia — a niche draw
Cyberpsychology grad program exists, suggesting some specialized academic offerings

Norfolk 757 area inspires strong regional pride among students and locals
No complaints about location; proximity to Hampton Roads seen as a plus

key_points: Band and performance culture is elite — nationally recognized, inspires genuine awe worldwide
Strong HBCU pride and community identity define the NSU experience
Small but loyal student community; campus life is real but not hyped on mainstream platforms

Active career pipeline events with major employers happening regularly on campus
